Project.Author: alessandramancas
Project.Description:
<html> <head> <style type="text/css"> <!-- body { color: #000000; font-family: Dialog; font-size: 12px } --> </style>
</head> <body> </body> </html>
Project.Name: warehouse_management

Class.Documentation:
A Constants class collects general used static constants.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A Constants class collects general used static constants.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: Constants

Attribute.Documentation:
Path to the initial application properties file. This constant can be referenced by

Springs Value annotation. Value is {@value}. appProps is declared in Springs XML

configuration.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: Path to the initial application properties file. This constant can be referenced by Springs Value annotation. Value is {@value}. appProps is declared in Springs XML configuration.
Attribute.Name: APPLICATION_INITIAL_PROPERTIES

Class.Documentation:
A CorePackage is a marker annotation to link to the current Java package. This is

useful for component scanning.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: CorePackage

Class.Documentation:
A DomainObject, implementation classes offer basic functionality characteristic to all

persisted domain objects.

Each domain object:

<ul>

<li>must have a field for optimistic locking purpose</li>

<li>must return whether it is a transient or persisted instance</li>

<li>must return the technical key value to the caller</li>

</ul>

@param <ID> Type of technical key class

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A DomainObject, implementation classes offer basic functionality characteristic to all persisted domain objects. Each domain object:
  • must have a field for optimistic locking purpose
  • must return whether it is a transient or persisted instance
  • must return the technical key value to the caller
@param Type of technical key class @author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: DomainObject

Operation.Documentation: Check whether the instance is a transient or persisted one.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Check whether the instance is a transient or persisted one.
Operation.Name: isNew
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: {@literal true} if transient (not persisted before), otherwise {@literal false}
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: {@literal true} if transient (not persisted before), otherwise {@literal false}

Operation.Documentation: Return the value of the optimistic locking field.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Return the value of the optimistic locking field.
Operation.Name: getVersion

Operation.Documentation: Return the technical key value.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Return the technical key value.
Operation.Name: getId

Class.Documentation:
A SecurityUtils collects useful functions regarding security aspects.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A SecurityUtils collects useful functions regarding security aspects.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: SecurityUtils

Operation.Documentation: With the given {@code username} and {@code password} create a Base64 encoded validhttp BASIC schema authorization header, and return it within a {@link HttpHeaders}object.
Operation.Documentation_plain: With the given {@code username} and {@code password} create a Base64 encoded validhttp BASIC schema authorization header, and return it within a {@link HttpHeaders}object.
Operation.Name: createHeaders
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: The HttpHeaders object containing the Authorization Header
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: The HttpHeaders object containing the Authorization Header

Class.Documentation:
A SpringProfiles is a collection of wellknown Spring Profiles used in OpenWMS.org.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A SpringProfiles is a collection of wellknown Spring Profiles used in OpenWMS.org.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: SpringProfiles

Attribute.Documentation:
Used to define that the service is running in a distributed environment.

@deprecated Use {@link org.ameba.app.SpringProfiles#DISTRIBUTED DISTRIBUTED} instead
Attribute.Documentation_plain:
Used to define that the service is running in a distributed environment.
@deprecated Use {@link org.ameba.app.SpringProfiles#DISTRIBUTED DISTRIBUTED} instead
Attribute.Name: DISTRIBUTED

Attribute.Documentation:
Used to define that asynchronous message handling is used.

@deprecated Use {@link org.ameba.app.SpringProfiles#AMQP AMQP} instead
Attribute.Documentation_plain:
Used to define that asynchronous message handling is used.
@deprecated Use {@link org.ameba.app.SpringProfiles#AMQP AMQP} instead
Attribute.Name: ASYNCHRONOUS_PROFILE

Attribute.Documentation: Used to define that the asynchronous message handling is NOT used.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: Used to define that the asynchronous message handling is NOT used.
Attribute.Name: NOT_ASYNCHRONOUS

Attribute.Documentation: Used to define that synchronous message handling is used.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: Used to define that synchronous message handling is used.
Attribute.Name: SYNCHRONOUS_PROFILE

Attribute.Documentation: Used to define application slices when the application's workflow is not managed by a supervisor process.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: Used to define application slices when the application's workflow is not managed by a supervisor process.
Attribute.Name: NOT_MANAGED

Attribute.Documentation: Used to define that synchronous message handling is used.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: Used to define that synchronous message handling is used.
Attribute.Name: IN_MEMORY

Attribute.Documentation:
Used to define that running within an OSGi container.

@deprecated In the next mayor release
Attribute.Documentation_plain:
Used to define that running within an OSGi container.
@deprecated In the next mayor release
Attribute.Name: OSGI

Attribute.Documentation:
Used to define that not running in an OSGi container.

@deprecated In the next mayor release
Attribute.Documentation_plain:
Used to define that not running in an OSGi container.
@deprecated In the next mayor release
Attribute.Name: NON_OSGI

Class.Documentation:
A FireAfterTransaction. is used to send a list of events synchronously after the current transaction has finished successfully.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A FireAfterTransaction. is used to send a list of events synchronously after the current transaction has finished successfully.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: FireAfterTransaction

Class.Documentation:
A FireAfterTransactionAsynchronous is used to send a list of events asynchronously after the current transaction has finished.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A FireAfterTransactionAsynchronous is used to send a list of events asynchronously after the current transaction has finished.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: FireAfterTransactionAsynchronous

Class.Documentation:
A Workflow annotated type is meant to be used in the BPMN workflow as bean.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A Workflow annotated type is meant to be used in the BPMN workflow as bean.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: Workflow

Class.Documentation:
An UserChangedEventAspect fires events after a method invocation completes.

It's main purpose is to fire events after a transaction succeeds, thereby the

advice must be enabled around Spring's Transaction advice.

<p>

Use the {@link FireAfterTransaction} event and declare some type of events

inside the <code>value</code> attribute. Instances of these events will then

be fired after the transaction completes.

</p>

Example: <blockquote>

<pre>

@FireAfterTransaction(events = { UserChangedEvent.class })

public User save(User user) { .. }

</pre>

</blockquote>

<p>

The component can be referenced by name {@value #COMPONENT_NAME}.

</p>

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
An UserChangedEventAspect fires events after a method invocation completes. It's main purpose is to fire events after a transaction succeeds, thereby the advice must be enabled around Spring's Transaction advice.

Use the {@link FireAfterTransaction} event and declare some type of events inside the value attribute. Instances of these events will then be fired after the transaction completes.

Example: 
@FireAfterTransaction(events = { UserChangedEvent.class })
public User save(User user) { .. }

The component can be referenced by name {@value #COMPONENT_NAME}.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: FireAfterTransactionAspect

Attribute.Documentation: Springs component name.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: Springs component name.
Attribute.Name: COMPONENT_NAME

Operation.Documentation: Only {@link ApplicationEvent}s are created and published over Springs{@link ApplicationContext}.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Only {@link ApplicationEvent}s are created and published over Springs{@link ApplicationContext}.
Operation.Name: fireEvent

Parameter.Documentation: The instance that is publishing the event
Parameter.Documentation_plain: The instance that is publishing the event
Parameter.Name: publisher

Parameter.Documentation: A list of event classes to fire
Parameter.Documentation_plain: A list of event classes to fire
Parameter.Name: events

Operation.Documentation: Only {@link ApplicationEvent}s are created and published over Springs{@link ApplicationContext}.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Only {@link ApplicationEvent}s are created and published over Springs{@link ApplicationContext}.
Operation.Name: fireEventAsync

Parameter.Documentation: The instance that is publishing the event
Parameter.Documentation_plain: The instance that is publishing the event
Parameter.Name: publisher

Parameter.Documentation: Stores a list of event classes to fire
Parameter.Documentation_plain: Stores a list of event classes to fire
Parameter.Name: events

Class.Documentation:
A ConfigurationChangedEvent is raised whenever OpenWMS {@code Preferences} change.

@author Heiko Scherrer

A ConfigurationChangedEvent is raised whenever OpenWMS {@code Preferences} change.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: ConfigurationChangedEvent

Operation.Documentation: Create a new ConfigurationChangedEvent.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Create a new ConfigurationChangedEvent.
Operation.Name: ConfigurationChangedEvent

Parameter.Documentation: The {@code Preferences} object that has changed or a Collection of those never {@literal null}
Parameter.Documentation_plain: The {@code Preferences} object that has changed or a Collection of those never {@literal null}
Parameter.Name: source

Class.Documentation:
A EventDispatcher.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A EventDispatcher.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: EventBroker

Class.Documentation:
An EventDispatcher dispatches / delivers events to subscribers.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
An EventDispatcher dispatches / delivers events to subscribers.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: EventDispatcher

Operation.Documentation: Take an event of type {@code T} and dispatch it to all subscribedlisteners.Note: It is not defined whether the event is delivered synchronously orasynchronously.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Take an event of type {@code T} and dispatch it to all subscribedlisteners.Note: It is not defined whether the event is delivered synchronously orasynchronously.
Operation.Name: dispatch

Class.Documentation:
A EventListener.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A EventListener.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: EventListener

Class.Documentation:
An EventPublisher publishes any type of {@link RootApplicationEvent}s to registered

{@code EventListener}s.

@author Heiko Scherrer

@see EventListener
Class.Name: EventPublisher

Operation.Documentation: Publish an event of type T to event listeners.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Publish an event of type T to event listeners.
Operation.Name: publish

Class.Documentation:
A RoleChangedEvent is fired to notify listeners about changes on an <code>Role</code> instance. A listener could probably evict a cache

of Roles.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: RoleChangedEvent

Operation.Documentation: Create a new RoleChangedEvent.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Create a new RoleChangedEvent.
Operation.Name: RoleChangedEvent

Class.Documentation:
A RootApplicationEvent is typically used as a super class for specific event types

within the application domain.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: RootApplicationEvent

Operation.Documentation: Create a new RootApplicationEvent.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Create a new RootApplicationEvent.

Class.Documentation:
A RootNotification is used as super class for notifications within the application

domain.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: RootNotification

Operation.Documentation: Constructs a RootNotification.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Constructs a RootNotification.
Operation.Name: RootNotification

Parameter.Documentation: Any serializable object to be transfered
Parameter.Documentation_plain: Any serializable object to be transfered
Parameter.Name: data

Operation.Documentation: Returns a String representation of this RootNotification.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns a String representation of this RootNotification.
Operation.Name: toString

Class.Documentation:
A SimpleEventDispatcher is a Spring managed component that stores all subscribers in an

in-memory key-value store implementation and calls all subscribers sequentially and

synchronously.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A SimpleEventDispatcher is a Spring managed component that stores all subscribers in an in-memory key-value store implementation and calls all subscribers sequentially and synchronously.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: SimpleEventDispatcher

Attribute.Documentation: Springs service name.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: Springs service name.
Attribute.Name: COMPONENT_NAME

Operation.Name: subscribe

Operation.Name: subscribe

Operation.Documentation: {@inheritDoc}
Operation.Name: unsubscribe

Operation.Documentation: {@inheritDoc}
Operation.Name: unsubscribe

Operation.Documentation: {@inheritDoc}
Operation.Name: dispatch

Class.Documentation:
An UserChangedEvent is raised to notify about changes on an {@code User} instance. A

listener may for instance evict a cache.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: UserChangedEvent

Operation.Documentation: Create a new UserChangedEvent.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Create a new UserChangedEvent.
Operation.Name: UserChangedEvent

Class.Documentation:
A DataNotFoundException is thrown to indicate that data was expected but nothing was

found.

Operation.Name: DataNotFoundException

Class.Documentation:
An ExceptionCodes class defines some, not all, of exception codes used in OpenWMS.org. The code is a descriptive message code, that can

be used for translation purpose.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: ExceptionCodes

Attribute.Documentation: Generic technical exception.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: Generic technical exception.
Attribute.Name: TECHNICAL_RT_ERROR

Attribute.Documentation: Thrown to signal one or more invalid parameters in API or method calls.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: Thrown to signal one or more invalid parameters in API or method calls.
Attribute.Name: INVALID_PARAMETER_ERROR

Attribute.Documentation: A generic validation error.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: A generic validation error.
Attribute.Name: VALIDATION_ERROR

Class.Documentation:
An IllegalConfigurationValueException is thrown to signal an invalid or unexpected

configured value, usually derived from a configuration file that is maintained by

developers or users.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
An IllegalConfigurationValueException is thrown to signal an invalid or unexpected configured value, usually derived from a configuration file that is maintained by developers or users.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: IllegalConfigurationValueException

Operation.Documentation: Create a new IllegalConfigurationValueException.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Create a new IllegalConfigurationValueException.
Operation.Name: IllegalConfigurationValueException

Operation.Documentation: Create a new IllegalConfigurationValueException.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Create a new IllegalConfigurationValueException.
Operation.Name: IllegalConfigurationValueException

Parameter.Documentation: the detail message. The detail message is saved for later retrieval by the {@link #getMessage()} method.
Parameter.Documentation_plain: the detail message. The detail message is saved for later retrieval by the {@link #getMessage()} method.
Parameter.Name: message

Operation.Documentation: Create a new IllegalConfigurationValueException.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Create a new IllegalConfigurationValueException.
Operation.Name: IllegalConfigurationValueException

Parameter.Documentation: the cause (which is saved for later retrieval by the {@link #getCause()} method). (A {@literal null} value is permitted, and indicates that the cause is nonexistentor unknown.)
Parameter.Documentation_plain: the cause (which is saved for later retrieval by the {@link #getCause()} method). (A {@literal null} value is permitted, and indicates that the cause is nonexistentor unknown.)
Parameter.Name: cause

Operation.Documentation: Create a new IllegalConfigurationValueException.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Create a new IllegalConfigurationValueException.
Operation.Name: IllegalConfigurationValueException

Parameter.Documentation: the detail message. The detail message is saved for later retrieval by the {@link #getMessage()} method.
Parameter.Documentation_plain: the detail message. The detail message is saved for later retrieval by the {@link #getMessage()} method.
Parameter.Name: message

Parameter.Documentation: the cause (which is saved for later retrieval by the {@link #getCause()} method). (A {@literal null} value is permitted, and indicates that the cause isnonexistent or unknown.)
Parameter.Documentation_plain: the cause (which is saved for later retrieval by the {@link #getCause()} method). (A {@literal null} value is permitted, and indicates that the cause isnonexistent or unknown.)
Parameter.Name: cause

Operation.Name: buildResponse

Operation.Documentation: Return the first element of list. If list is {@literal null} of empty,{@literal null} is returned.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Return the first element of list. If list is {@literal null} of empty,{@literal null} is returned.
Operation.Name: getFirstOrNull
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: list[0] or {@literal null} if list == null || list.isEmpty
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: list[0] or {@literal null} if list == null || list.isEmpty

Operation.Documentation: Convert a list into a {@link LinkedHashMap} using a {@link ListExtractor} todetermine the keys and values for the new Map.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Convert a list into a {@link LinkedHashMap} using a {@link ListExtractor} todetermine the keys and values for the new Map.
Operation.Name: asHashMap
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: A created {@link LinkedHashMap} or an empty map when one of the arguments is {@literal null}
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: A created {@link LinkedHashMap} or an empty map when one of the arguments is {@literal null}

Parameter.Documentation: To determine key and value for each list entry
Parameter.Documentation_plain: To determine key and value for each list entry
Parameter.Name: extractor

Class.Documentation:
A ListExtractor.

@param <K> Type of key

@param <V> Type of value

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: ListExtractor

Operation.Documentation: From a list node of type {@code T} extract the key element.
Operation.Documentation_plain: From a list node of type {@code T} extract the key element.
Operation.Name: extractKey

Parameter.Documentation: The node to extract the key from
Parameter.Documentation_plain: The node to extract the key from
Parameter.Name: listNode

Operation.Documentation: From a list node of type {@code T} extract the value.
Operation.Documentation_plain: From a list node of type {@code T} extract the value.
Operation.Name: extractValue

Parameter.Documentation: The node to extract the value from
Parameter.Documentation_plain: The node to extract the value from
Parameter.Name: listNode

Class.Documentation:
An OnRemovalListener is able to hook in the lifecycle of an entity class. A class

implementing this interface is called before an Entity of type {@code T} is removed and

can prevent the deletion.

@param <T> Any kind of {@link BaseEntity}

@author <a href="mailto:russelltina@users.sourceforge.net">Tina Russell</a>
Class.Name: OnRemovalListener

Operation.Documentation: Decide whether the {@code entity} can be removed or not.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Decide whether the {@code entity} can be removed or not.
Operation.Name: preRemove
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: {@code true} if removal is allowed, otherwise {@code false}
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: {@code true} if removal is allowed, otherwise {@code false}

Class.Documentation:
A RemovalNotAllowedException is thrown when the caller is not allowed to remove an

entity.

@author <a href="mailto:russelltina@users.sourceforge.net">Tina Russell</a>
Class.Name: RemovalNotAllowedException

Operation.Documentation: Create a new RemovalNotAllowedException.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Create a new RemovalNotAllowedException.
Operation.Name: RemovalNotAllowedException

Class.Documentation:
A ApplicationInitializer is used to determine the runtime environment OpenWMS is

running in. In case of an OSGi server, like Spring dmServer, the expected Spring

profile is {@link SpringProfiles#OSGI}. If not running in an OSGi environment, the

profile {@link SpringProfiles#NON_OSGI} is activated - if not already set.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A ApplicationInitializer is used to determine the runtime environment OpenWMS is running in. In case of an OSGi server, like Spring dmServer, the expected Spring profile is {@link SpringProfiles#OSGI}. If not running in an OSGi environment, the profile {@link SpringProfiles#NON_OSGI} is activated - if not already set.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: ApplicationInitializer

Operation.Documentation: {@inheritDoc}<p>Depending on the underlying platform, different Spring profiles are included.
Operation.Documentation_plain:
{@inheritDoc}

Depending on the underlying platform, different Spring profiles are included.
Operation.Name: initialize

Class.Documentation:
A LocalServiceInitializer raises {@link MergePropertiesEvent}s whenever the OpenWMS

OSGi bundle is started or restarted.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: LocalServiceInitializer

Operation.Documentation: {@inheritDoc}
Operation.Name: onApplicationEvent

Class.Documentation:
A TimeProvider defines several widely used time formats as constants and provides the current time. An implementation may be loaded using

the Java1.6 ServiceLoader facility or by Spring dependency injection.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: TimeProvider

Attribute.Documentation: The format pattern for all date types.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: The format pattern for all date types.
Attribute.Name: DATE_FORMAT

Attribute.Documentation: The format pattern for all date-time types without milliseconds and without timezone.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: The format pattern for all date-time types without milliseconds and without timezone.
Attribute.Name: DATE_TIME_FORMAT

Attribute.Documentation: The format pattern for all date-time with milliseconds without timezone types.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: The format pattern for all date-time with milliseconds without timezone types.
Attribute.Name: DATE_TIME_MILLIS_FORMAT

Attribute.Documentation: The format pattern for all date-time types without milliseconds but with timezone.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: The format pattern for all date-time types without milliseconds but with timezone.
Attribute.Name: DATE_TIME_WITH_TIMEZONE_FORMAT

Attribute.Documentation: The format pattern for all date-time types with milliseconds and timezone.
Attribute.Documentation_plain: The format pattern for all date-time types with milliseconds and timezone.
Attribute.Name: DATE_TIME_MILLIS_WITH_TIMEZONE_FORMAT

Operation.Documentation: Returns the current date and time considering the configured timezone.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the current date and time considering the configured timezone.
Operation.Name: nowAsCurrentDate

Operation.Documentation: Returns the current date and time in UTC timezone.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the current date and time in UTC timezone.
Operation.Name: nowAsZuluDate

Operation.Documentation: Returns the current date and time considering the configured timezone.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the current date and time considering the configured timezone.
Operation.Name: nowAsCurrentZonedDateTime

Operation.Documentation: Returns the current date and time considering the configured timezone.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the current date and time considering the configured timezone.
Operation.Name: nowAsZuluZonedDateTime

Operation.Documentation: Returns the current date and time considering the given timezone.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the current date and time considering the given timezone.
Operation.Name: nowAsZonedDateTime

Operation.Documentation: Returns the current date and time of the system considering the configured timezone.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the current date and time of the system considering the configured timezone.
Operation.Name: nowAsCurrentInstant

Operation.Documentation: Returns the date and time in UTC timezone.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the date and time in UTC timezone.
Operation.Name: nowAsZuluInstant

Class.Documentation:
A TreeNode to define a tree data structure.

@param <T> The type of node.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: TreeNode

Operation.Documentation: Get the data of the node.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Get the data of the node.
Operation.Name: getData

Operation.Documentation: Set the data of the node.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Set the data of the node.
Operation.Name: setData

Operation.Documentation: Return whether this node is leaf.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Return whether this node is leaf.
Operation.Name: isLeaf
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: {@literal true} if this node is leaf, otherwise {@literal false}
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: {@literal true} if this node is leaf, otherwise {@literal false}

Operation.Documentation: Get all child entries. Each child entry contains an identifier (key) and a childnode (value).Identifiers are used to generate model keys representing paths to tree nodes. Modelkeys are used:<ul><li>For persisting tree state. That means identifiers should be serializable whensome JSF features are used, e.g. client-side state saving.</li><li>For construction of client identifiers. Client identifier for tree nodesconsists of String representations of identifiers separated by a separatorchar. A String representation of identifier should be a valid XML ID, e.g.conform to <a href="http://www.w3.org/TR/xml11/#NT-Name"> XML Name Production</a>production.</li></ul>
Operation.Documentation_plain:
Get all child entries. Each child entry contains an identifier (key) and a childnode (value).Identifiers are used to generate model keys representing paths to tree nodes. Modelkeys are used:
  • For persisting tree state. That means identifiers should be serializable whensome JSF features are used, e.g. client-side state saving.
  • For construction of client identifiers. Client identifier for tree nodesconsists of String representations of identifiers separated by a separatorchar. A String representation of identifier should be a valid XML ID, e.g.conform to XML Name Productionproduction.
Operation.Name: getChildren
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: {@link Iterator} over Map.Entry instances containing {@link TreeNode} as values and their identifiers as keys
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: {@link Iterator} over Map.Entry instances containing {@link TreeNode} as values and their identifiers as keys

Operation.Documentation: Find a child by id.See {@link #getChildren()} for more information about the identifier constraints.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Find a child by id.See {@link #getChildren()} for more information about the identifier constraints.
Operation.Name: getChild

Parameter.Documentation: The identifier of the child to find
Parameter.Documentation_plain: The identifier of the child to find
Parameter.Name: id

Operation.Documentation: Adds a child to the children.See {@link #getChildren()} for more information about identifier constraints.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Adds a child to the children.See {@link #getChildren()} for more information about identifier constraints.
Operation.Name: addChild

Operation.Documentation: Removes a child from the children collection.See {@link #getChildren()} for more information about identifier constraints.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Removes a child from the children collection.See {@link #getChildren()} for more information about identifier constraints.
Operation.Name: removeChild

Parameter.Documentation: The id of the child to remove
Parameter.Documentation_plain: The id of the child to remove
Parameter.Name: id

Operation.Documentation: Get the parent {@link TreeNode}.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Get the parent {@link TreeNode}.
Operation.Name: getParent
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: parent {@link TreeNode} instance or {@literal null} if the node is the root
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: parent {@link TreeNode} instance or {@literal null} if the node is the root

Operation.Documentation: Set the parent {@link TreeNode}.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Set the parent {@link TreeNode}.
Operation.Name: setParent

Parameter.Documentation: {@link TreeNode} to set as parent
Parameter.Name: parent

Class.Documentation:
A TreeNodeImpl is a simple implementation of a {@link TreeNode}.

@param <T> The type of the node

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: TreeNodeImpl

Operation.Documentation: {@inheritDoc}
Operation.Name: getChild

Operation.Documentation: {@inheritDoc}
Operation.Name: addChild

Operation.Documentation: {@inheritDoc}
Operation.Name: removeChild

Operation.Documentation: {@inheritDoc}
Operation.Name: getChildren

Operation.Documentation: {@inheritDoc}
Operation.Name: isLeaf

Class.Documentation:
A AbstractBaseUnit is a stereotype interface that can be used in wildcard boundaries

for any kind of BaseUnit types.

@param <T> The type of BaseUnit

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: AbstractBaseUnit

Class.Documentation:
A definition of any kind of measurement used in the application. In general an

{@code AbstractMeasure} is the base class for all kind of measures. For example {@literal 42 grams}

is a {@code AbstractMeasure} (in particular a {@code Weight}, whereas 42 is the

magnitude and grams is the {@code BaseUnit}. This type is merely used to erase the

type declaration.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: AbstractMeasure

Class.Documentation:
A BaseUnit is the type definition of an {@code Measurable}. Each BaseUnit defines a

base {@code Measurable}. For example the BaseUnit of weights can define grams (G),

kilograms (KG) or tons (T) and 1G is the base unit.

@param <T> Concrete type of BaseUnit

@author Heiko Scherrer

@GlossaryTerm
Class.Name: BaseUnit

Operation.Documentation: Return all sub types of the {@code UnitType}.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Return all sub types of the {@code UnitType}.
Operation.Name: getAll

Operation.Documentation: Return the base unit type of the {@code UnitType}.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Return the base unit type of the {@code UnitType}.
Operation.Name: getBaseUnit

Class.Documentation:
A CoreTypeDefinitions.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A CoreTypeDefinitions.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: CoreTypeDefinitions

Attribute.Documentation: Length of a Description field: {@value}
Attribute.Documentation_plain: Length of a Description field: {@value}
Attribute.Name: DESCRIPTION_LENGTH

Attribute.Documentation: Length of a Quantity field: {@value}
Attribute.Documentation_plain: Length of a Quantity field: {@value}
Attribute.Name: QUANTITY_LENGTH

Class.Documentation:
An ImageProvider stores an image file.

@author <a href="mailto:russelltina@users.sourceforge.net">Tina Russell</a>
Class.Documentation_plain: An ImageProvider stores an image file. @author Tina Russell
Class.Name: ImageProvider

Operation.Documentation: Return the image of the {@code User}.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Return the image of the {@code User}.
Operation.Name: getImage

Operation.Documentation: Change the image of the {@code User}.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Change the image of the {@code User}.
Operation.Name: setImage

Class.Documentation:
A Measurable.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A Measurable.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: Measurable

Operation.Documentation: Returns the type of {@code Measurable}.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the type of {@code Measurable}.
Operation.Name: getUnitType

Operation.Documentation: Get the magnitude of this {@code Measurable}.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Get the magnitude of this {@code Measurable}.
Operation.Name: getMagnitude

Operation.Documentation: Check whether the magnitude is 0.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Check whether the magnitude is 0.
Operation.Name: isZero
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: {@literal true} is magnitude is 0, otherwise {@literal false}
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: {@literal true} is magnitude is 0, otherwise {@literal false}

Operation.Documentation: Check whether the magnitude is of negative value.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Check whether the magnitude is of negative value.
Operation.Name: isNegative
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: {@literal true} if the magnitude is of negative value, otherwise {@literal false}
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: {@literal true} if the magnitude is of negative value, otherwise {@literal false}

Operation.Documentation: Convert this {@code Measurable} into another {@code Measurable} .
Operation.Documentation_plain: Convert this {@code Measurable} into another {@code Measurable} .
Operation.Name: convertTo

Class.Documentation:
A PriorityLevel is used to prioritize arbitrary kind of orders.

@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Documentation_plain:
A PriorityLevel is used to prioritize arbitrary kind of orders.
@author Heiko Scherrer
Class.Name: PriorityLevel

Operation.Documentation: Framework constructor.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Framework constructor.
Operation.Name: PriorityLevel

Operation.Documentation: Return the {@code PriorityLevel} of the given {@code priority}.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Return the {@code PriorityLevel} of the given {@code priority}.
Operation.Name: of
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation:
Enum value

@throws IllegalArgumentException if String value does not match one of the enum values
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: Enum value @throws IllegalArgumentException if String value does not match one of the enum values

Operation.Documentation: Convert the priority level as integer range into the corresponding enumeration.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Convert the priority level as integer range into the corresponding enumeration.
Operation.Name: convert

Operation.Documentation: Initializing constructor.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Initializing constructor.
Operation.Name: PriorityLevel

Operation.Documentation: Get the order.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Get the order.
Operation.Name: getOrder
